chain.thought: Explanation: Team Europe wins when their score (the first number) is higher than Team USA’s score (the second number). Checking each row: Europe lost in rows 1 (217 < 279), 2 (217 < 244), 4 (206 < 275), 5 (176 < 258), 6 (202 < 203), 7 (202 < 264), and 8 (196 < 235). Europe won only in row 3 (210 > 199). Therefore, Team Europe won 1 match.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
Reviewing each match's scores:
- Match 25: Europe 217 vs USA 279 — USA wins.
- Match 26: Europe 217 vs USA 244 — USA wins.
- Match 27: Europe 210 vs USA 199 — Europe wins.
- Match 28: Europe 206 vs USA 275 — USA wins.
- Match 29: Europe 176 vs USA 258 — USA wins.
- Match 30 (Baker): Europe 202 vs USA 203 — USA wins.
- Match 31: Europe 202 vs USA 264 — USA wins.
- Match 32: Europe 196 vs USA 235 — USA wins.

Team Europe won only match 27, so they won 1 match.
